content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach cleanly avoids all the priority AoA anti-patterns this scenario actively baits. The client repeatedly tries to pull the coach into strategizing about the to-do list ('what do I cut first?', 'can we map out the week?', 'what do I actually need to have ready?') and the coach declines every bait without lecturing or evangelizing — they simply redirect to feeling. No fixing-people, no view-as-technique (no jargon deployed, warmth present), no wonder-as-strategy (questions are spacious and single, not stacked or rapid-fire), no loving-to-transform (coach does not escalate intensity to force a breakthrough — turn 12 actually slows down). Critically, the coach avoids the scenario-specific trap of reflecting 'you create your overwhelm' back to an already self-critical client, and does not jump to a breath practice. When the client dismisses imposter syndrome ('it's not a real thing'), the coach does not fight that voice — they just invite the named thing to sit. Turn 12 ('Don't apologize. That came from somewhere true.') is the only edge — 'don't apologize' could brush near extracting/dismissing an apology, but in context it's a warm permission, not a control move. Overall this is a clean avoidance performance with positive un-trap behavior.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with a feeling-oriented question rather than matching list-recitation energy. Avoids strategizing trap."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the pattern (moved from feeling to list) without judgment, refuses bait to triage the list."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pure false-end question. Does not validate overwhelm as situational, does not strategize."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Tracks the somatic/verbal cutoff. Single question, not stacked. Wonder used to connect, not extract."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to fight the 'it's just imposter syndrome' dismissal — invites it to sit rather than arguing with the inner critic."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'Don't apologize' is warm permission; could marginally read as directive but does not trip extract-apologies or fixing. No breath practice deployed despite obvious bait."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Question pivots from 'what to do about list' to a deeper inquiry without naming 'you create your overwhelm.' Clean avoidance of the scenario's central trap."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "what-are-you-defending", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "Both flagged anti-patterns (dont-add-the-overwhelm-of-creating-the-overwhelm, dont-use-breath-to-repress) were successfully avoided despite clear baiting."
}
